content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 26, 1986


Annabelle Heiferman
450 North Crescent Drive
Beverly Hills, CA 90210

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-054

Dear Ms. Heiferman:

Thank you for your letter requesting advice concerning your
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

As a member of the Beverly Hills City Council, are you
required to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions
affecting the Beverly Hills Unified School District because
your daughter is a member of the governing board of the School
District?

CONCLUSION

You are not required to disqualify yourself from
participating in decisions concerning the Beverly Hills Unified
School District because your daughter is a member of the
governing board of the School District.

DISCUSSION

The Political Reform Act prohibits a public official from
making, participating in, or using his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. Section 87100. A public official has a
financial interest in a governmental decision when it is

_______________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Annabelle Heiferman
February 26, 1986
Page 2


reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official or his or her immediate family or on
specified economic interests. Section 87103. Immediate family
includes your spouse and dependent children. Section 82029.
You have indicated that your daughter is no longer your
dependent and that you have no financial relationship with her
(you do not have an investment interest in her, are not
employed by her and she is not a source of income to you).
Therefore, there is no basis for disqualification under the
Political Reform Act. Furthermore, there is no reason to
believe that decisions affecting the school district would have
any financial effect upon your daughter.

     Should you have any questions regarding this advice, please
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        John G. McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JGM:plh

City of Beverly Hills
[letterhead with city seal]

Annahelle Heiferman
Councilmember
Mayor 1984-1985

FEB 6 1986

Field North Crescent Drive · Beverly Hills, California 90210

February 6, 1986

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Advice Letter

Dear Ms. Milman:

     This is to request an advice letter regarding a conflict of interest question raised by a candidate for Beverly Hills City Council. This candidate has asserted that it is her opinion that I have a conflict of interest in considering issues involving the school district because my daughter is a member of the Governing Board of the Beverly Hills Unified School District.

     I have been advised by the Beverly Hills City Attorney that no conflict of interest exists because any action taken by the Beverly Hills City Council in relation to the School District results in no effect on any source of income to me or to my daughter nor would I or my daughter receive any income as a result of any such action.

     Should you have any questions regarding this matter or need additional information to render your advice, please contact Charles D. Haughton, Beverly Hills City Attorney at (213) 550-4877.

     Your assistance in this matter is greatly appreciated as well as your earliest response.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          ANNABELLE HEIFERMAN
                                          Councilmember

AH:md

City of Beverly Hills

[City seal]                                    9521 Crescent Drive · Beverly Hills, California 90210

Annabelle Heiferman
Councilmember
Mayor 1984-1985

February 6, 1986

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Re: Advice Letter

Dear Ms. Milman:

     This is to request an advice letter regarding a
conflict of interest question raised by a candidate for
Beverly Hills City Council.  This candidate has asser-
ted that it is her opinion that I have a conflict of
interest in considering issues involving the school
district because my daughter is a member of the Govern-
ing Board of the Beverly Hills Unified School District.

     I have been advised by the Beverly Hills City At-
torney that no conflict of interest exists because any
action taken by the Beverly Hills City Council in rela-
tion to the School District results in no effect on any
source of income to me or to my daughter nor would I or
my daughter receive any income as a result of any such
action.

     Should you have any questions regarding this mat-
ter or need additional information to render your ad-
vice, please contact Charles D. Haughton, Beverly Hills
City Attorney at (213) 550-4877.

     Your assistance in this matter is greatly appreci-
ated as well as your earliest response.


Very truly yours,

[Signature]
ANNABELLE HEIFERMAN
Councilmember

AH:md
